Restore the last approved IAM baseline for the affected principal.
verification_steps: Re-run the drift detector and confirm the change no longer appears.
Test the principal and verify only approved actions still succeed.

advice.summary: test now shows drift under USER_ADDED_WITH_POLICIES; confirm the permissions match an approved business need.
possible_impact: Compliance and incident-response impact is likely immediate.
Potential full account compromise or privilege escalation.
Resource-wide scope can affect production assets across environments.
Sensitive data exposure and destructive actions may occur quickly.
Wildcard actions can be abused for rapid privilege expansion.

policy_impact_analysis.policy_summary: Grants access across service(s): rds. Includes wildcard action scope. Applies to all resources.
abuse_paths: Broad wildcard scope can be abused for lateral movement.
possible_environment_effects: May allow broad operations including unintended administrative actions.
Could affect production resources account-wide if misused.
targeted_mitigations: Limit service scope to required APIs only: rds.
Replace wildcard actions with explicit allow-lists.
Replace Resource '*' with specific ARNs or strict tag conditions.

policy_impact_analysis.policy_summary: Grants access across service(s): iam. Includes wildcard action scope. Applies to all resources.
abuse_paths: Privilege escalation by creating/modifying IAM identities and policies.
possible_environment_effects: May allow broad operations including unintended administrative actions.
Could affect production resources account-wide if misused.
May enable identity and permission tampering in the environment.
targeted_mitigations: Limit service scope to required APIs only: iam.
Replace wildcard actions with explicit allow-lists.
Replace Resource '*' with specific ARNs or strict tag conditions.
Apply permissions boundary/SCP guardrails to block IAM escalation operations.

policy_impact_analysis.policy_summary: Policy permissions should be reviewed for least privilege.
possible_environment_effects: Could expand operational access beyond intended scope.
targeted_mitigations: Restrict policy to least privilege and document approved use-case.

recommended_actions: Verify the policy change against the intended ticket or change record.
Remove wildcard or unnecessary permissions before promoting the change.
Assign clear ownership so future drift can be reviewed quickly.
Restrict IAM permissions to explicit actions (for example iam:Get*, iam:List*) and remove iam:*.
Apply a permissions boundary to the principal to block privilege escalation paths.
Scope EC2 actions to approved instance, volume, and network resource ARNs or tags.
Limit S3 access to required bucket ARN prefixes and block account-wide s3:* patterns.
Restrict Lambda actions to required functions and disallow wildcard invoke/update permissions.
Constrain RDS actions to approved DB instance/cluster ARNs and remove broad administrative actions.
Replace wildcard action grants with minimal action allow-lists per service.
Replace Resource '*' with exact ARNs or tag-based conditions.
rollback_steps: Revert the principal to the previous approved policy set.
Remove any policy versions or attachments added during the drift event.
verification_steps: Refresh the dashboard data and confirm the policy warning count drops as expected.
Validate that the principal can access only the intended AWS resources.

recommended_actions: Check that the principal still needs every added permission.
Reduce the scope to exact actions and resources instead of broad access.
Document why the change exists and when it should be revisited.
Restrict IAM permissions to explicit actions (for example iam:Get*, iam:List*) and remove iam:*.
Apply a permissions boundary to the principal to block privilege escalation paths.
Replace wildcard action grants with minimal action allow-lists per service.
Replace Resource '*' with exact ARNs or tag-based conditions.
rollback_steps: Back out the new permissions and re-apply the known good baseline.
Re-check group or user attachments to confirm the rollback took effect.
verification_steps: Run drift detection again to verify the environment returned to baseline.
Confirm no extra managed or inline policies remain attached.
